actors.name: User
actors.description: An individual or entity interacting with the Superfluid protocol, such as sending/receiving streams, wrapping/unwrapping tokens, or participating in distributions.
capabilities: Wrap/Unwrap Super Tokens
Create/Update/Delete money streams (flows)
Send/Receive Super Tokens
Participate in distributions as a member
Act as a distributor for a pool
Deploy Super Apps or other smart contracts interacting with Superfluid
Schedule streams or vesting

actors.name: Super App
actors.description: A smart contract registered with the Superfluid Protocol, designed to react to Superfluid agreement actions (e.g., stream creation/update/deletion) via callbacks.
capabilities: React to stream events via callbacks
Execute custom logic based on Superfluid agreement state changes
Manage Super Token flows programmatically
Integrate Superfluid functionalities into dApps

actors.name: Pool Admin
actors.description: An account responsible for managing a specific Distribution Pool, including assigning units to members and configuring pool parameters.
capabilities: Create Distribution Pools
Assign/Update units for pool members
Manage pool configurations
Can execute fund distributions (if not set to distributionFromAnyAddress)

actors.name: Sentinel
actors.description: A node monitoring the Superfluid network, specifically Constant Flow Agreements, to close critical or insolvent streams and earn rewards.
capabilities: Monitor streams for solvency
Close critical or insolvent streams
Participate in the TOGA mechanism (potentially as PIC)

actors.name: PIC (Patrician in Charge)
actors.description: An account that has staked tokens in the TOGA contract to become the primary liquidator for a specific Super Token, earning rewards for closing streams during the patrician period.
capabilities: Stake tokens in TOGA to become PIC
Receive rewards for closing streams during the patrician period
Run sentinel software to monitor and liquidate streams

actors.name: Superfluid DAO Participant
actors.description: A member of the Superfluid DAO, typically holding SUP tokens, participating in the governance of the protocol.
capabilities: Vote on governance proposals
Submit governance proposals (potentially)
Influence protocol upgrades and parameter changes

actors.name: Flow Operator
actors.description: An account authorized by a token holder to manage their Superfluid flows (create, update, delete) up to a certain allowance.
capabilities: Create flows on behalf of another account
Update flows on behalf of another account
Delete flows on behalf of another account
Operate within a granted flow rate allowance

actors.name: Off-chain Automation Service
actors.description: Represents various off-chain services that interact with Superfluid contracts to automate tasks like auto-wrapping, stream scheduling, or vesting.
capabilities: Monitor on-chain conditions
Trigger on-chain transactions based on predefined logic (e.g., wrap tokens, start/end streams)

assets.name: Super Token
assets.description: The primary asset type used in all Superfluid operations. Can be a wrapped ERC20, a Pure Super Token, or a Native Asset Super Token. Enables functionalities like streaming and distributions.

assets.name: Underlying ERC20 Token
assets.description: A standard ERC20 token that is wrapped to create a Super Token (Wrapper Super Token). It is locked in the wrapper contract while the Super Token circulates.

assets.name: Flow (Money Stream)
assets.description: A continuous, second-by-second transfer of Super Tokens from a sender to a receiver, defined by a flow rate.

assets.name: Distribution Pool Units
assets.description: Shares denoting an account's proportional claim on distributions from a specific Superfluid Distribution Pool.

assets.name: Stream Buffer/Deposit
assets.description: Super Tokens temporarily locked by a stream sender as a security deposit to ensure stream solvency during critical periods.

assets.name: TOGA Stake
assets.description: Funds locked in the TOGA (Transparent Ongoing Auction) contract by an account aspiring to be or currently acting as the PIC (Patrician in Charge) for a Super Token.

assets.name: User Data
assets.description: Arbitrary data that can be included with Superfluid function calls, often utilized by Super App callbacks or off-chain systems.

assets.name: SUP Token
assets.description: The governance token for the Superfluid protocol, used for voting and participating in the DAO.

components.name: Superfluid Host Contract
components.description: The central smart contract of the Superfluid Protocol. It manages Super Token registrations, agreement registrations (like CFA and GDA), Super App registrations, and facilitates callbacks to Super Apps. It is a UUPS Proxy.

components.name: Super Token Contract
components.description: An instance of a Super Token (Wrapper, Pure, or Native). It extends ERC20 functionality to support Superfluid agreements like streaming and distributions. It is a UUPS Proxy.

components.name: Constant Flow Agreement (CFAv1)
components.description: A Superfluid agreement smart contract that enables the creation, update, and deletion of continuous money streams (flows) of Super Tokens. It is a UUPS Proxy.

components.name: General Distribution Agreement (GDAv1)
components.description: A Superfluid agreement smart contract that enables one-to-many or many-to-many distributions of Super Tokens, either instantly or via streams, through Distribution Pools. It is a UUPS Proxy.

components.name: Super Token Factory Contract
components.description: A smart contract used to permissionlessly deploy new Super Token contracts (Wrapper or Pure). It is a UUPS Proxy.

components.name: Distribution Pool Contract
components.description: A smart contract instance representing a specific pool for GDA distributions, managed by a Pool Admin. It is a UUPS Proxy.

components.name: TOGA (Transparent Ongoing Auction) Contract
components.description: A smart contract managing the auction for PIC (Patrician in Charge) roles and their stakes, crucial for the stream liquidation mechanism.

components.name: ACL (Access Control List) Mechanism
components.description: A feature within the Superfluid protocol (primarily CFA) allowing accounts to grant permissions to Flow Operators to manage their streams.

components.name: SuperTokenV1Library
components.description: A Solidity library providing helper functions for developers to interact with Super Tokens and Superfluid agreements within their smart contracts.

components.name: CFAv1Forwarder Contract
components.description: An immutable smart contract designed to simplify client-side (e.g., dApp frontend) interactions with the Constant Flow Agreement (CFA).

components.name: GDAv1Forwarder Contract
components.description: An immutable smart contract designed to simplify client-side (e.g., dApp frontend) interactions with the General Distribution Agreement (GDA) and its pools.

components.name: Superfluid Subgraph
components.description: An off-chain indexing service that provides queryable data about Superfluid protocol activities across various networks.

components.name: Auto-Wrap System
components.description: An automated system (likely off-chain bot + smart contract interaction) that wraps ERC20 tokens into Super Tokens when a user's Super Token balance is low to maintain active streams.

components.name: Stream Scheduler System
components.description: A feature (likely off-chain bot + smart contract interaction) enabling users to schedule the start and/or end of Superfluid streams.

components.name: Vesting Scheduler System
components.description: A smart contract system (potentially with off-chain automation) for setting up token vesting schedules with cliffs and linear Superfluid streams.

components.name: MacroForwarder Contract
components.description: A trusted forwarder contract that allows users to execute pre-defined batch operations (macros) on the Superfluid protocol in a single transaction.

components.name: Resolver Contract
components.description: A contract assisting in locating all protocol constituent contract addresses. (doc_1, doc_61)

components.name: SuperfluidGovernanceII Contract
components.description: The main governance contract that will allow Superfluid community members to participate in the governance of the protocol. It is a UUPS Proxy. (doc_61)

components.name: ConstantFlowNFT Contract
components.description: Smart contract for non-fungible tokens (NFTs) that have a constant flow rate associated with them. These are NFTs minted to represent Money Streaming flows. It is a UUPS Proxy. (doc_61)

components.name: PoolAdminNFT Contract
components.description: NFT contract representing admin rights over a SuperfluidPool. It is a UUPS Proxy. (doc_61)

components.name: PoolMemberNFT Contract
components.description: This is an NFT contract where tokens represent membership within a pool, granting units to members in that pool. It is a UUPS Proxy. (doc_61)

data_flows.data: Create/Update/Delete Flow parameters (token, receiver, flowRate, userData)
data_flows.description: User initiates a stream operation via the CFAv1Forwarder.

data_flows.data: Formatted call to CFA (e.g., createFlow, updateFlow, deleteFlow)
data_flows.description: CFAv1Forwarder forwards the stream operation to the Superfluid Host.
Superfluid Host calls the Constant Flow Agreement to execute the stream operation.

data_flows.data: Balance update instructions for Super Token
data_flows.description: CFA updates sender/receiver balances and stream state in the Super Token contract.
User requests to wrap underlying ERC20 tokens into Super Tokens or unwrap Super Tokens back to ERC20.

data_flows.data: Update Member Units parameters (pool, member, newUnits, userData)
data_flows.description: Pool Admin updates member units in a Distribution Pool via GDAv1Forwarder.
GDAv1Forwarder forwards the pool operation to the Superfluid Host.
Superfluid Host calls the General Distribution Agreement to execute the pool operation.

data_flows.data: Unit update instructions for Distribution Pool
data_flows.description: GDA updates member units in the specific Distribution Pool contract.

data_flows.data: Liquidation transaction for a critical stream
data_flows.description: Sentinel submits a transaction to liquidate a stream.
Superfluid Host invokes a callback on a registered Super App when a relevant agreement action occurs.

data_flows.data: New context or further agreement calls
data_flows.description: Super App responds to a callback, potentially making further calls to Superfluid agreements via the Host.
User or dApp queries the Superfluid Subgraph for protocol data.

data_flows.data: Token-related events (e.g., Transfer, Approval, TokenUpgraded, TokenDowngraded)
data_flows.description: Super Token contract emits events indexed by the Superfluid Subgraph.
CFAv1 contract emits events indexed by the Superfluid Subgraph.
GDAv1 contract emits events indexed by the Superfluid Subgraph.

data_flows.data: Pool-specific events (e.g., MemberUnitsUpdated, DistributionClaimed from pool context)
data_flows.description: Distribution Pool contract emits events indexed by the Superfluid Subgraph.
Superfluid Host contract emits events indexed by the Superfluid Subgraph.

trust_boundaries.name: User Wallet - Protocol Interface
trust_boundaries.description: Separates the user's private key environment from the Superfluid protocol's on-chain contracts. Protects user assets from direct unauthorized access by the protocol.

trust_boundaries.name: Forwarder Contract - Superfluid Host
trust_boundaries.description: Separates immutable forwarder contracts from the upgradable Superfluid Host. Forwarders are trusted to correctly format and relay calls.

trust_boundaries.name: Superfluid Host - Agreement Contracts
trust_boundaries.description: The Host is trusted to call the correct, registered agreement contracts (CFA, GDA).

trust_boundaries.name: Superfluid Host - Super App
trust_boundaries.description: The Host invokes callbacks on registered Super Apps. Super Apps are third-party contracts and their behavior is not controlled by Superfluid core protocol, though registration may be permissioned.

trust_boundaries.name: On-chain Protocol - Off-chain Services
trust_boundaries.description: Separates the deterministic on-chain Superfluid protocol from off-chain services like Sentinels, Subgraphs, or Automation Bots. Off-chain services read on-chain state and may submit transactions.

trust_boundaries.name: User Wallet - Super Token Contract (Wrap/Unwrap)
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary governing the transfer of custody of Underlying ERC20 Tokens from the User to the Super Token Contract during wrapping, and Super Tokens from the User to the contract for unwrapping. The integrity of the wrapper contract ensures 1:1 backing and correct handling of these transfers.

behaviors.name: User Creates Money Stream
behaviors.description: User initiates a transaction to create a new money stream of a Super Token to a specified receiver at a given flow rate.

behaviors.name: User Wraps ERC20 to Super Token
behaviors.description: User approves the Super Token contract and calls 'upgrade' to convert their ERC20 tokens into Super Tokens.

behaviors.name: User Unwraps Super Token to ERC20
behaviors.description: User calls 'downgrade' on the Super Token contract to convert their Super Tokens back into the underlying ERC20 tokens.

behaviors.name: Pool Admin Creates Distribution Pool
behaviors.description: Pool Admin creates a new Distribution Pool for a specific Super Token, defining initial configurations.

behaviors.name: Pool Admin Updates Member Units
behaviors.description: Pool Admin updates the number of units a member holds in a Distribution Pool, affecting their share of distributions.

behaviors.name: Distributor Sends Tokens to Pool
behaviors.description: A distributor (can be any user if pool allows) sends Super Tokens to a Distribution Pool for instant or streaming distribution to members.

behaviors.name: Sentinel Liquidates Critical Stream
behaviors.description: A Sentinel node identifies a stream where the sender's balance is zero (critical) and submits a transaction to close it, potentially earning a reward.

behaviors.name: PIC Stakes in TOGA
behaviors.description: An account stakes tokens in the TOGA contract to become the Patrician in Charge for a specific Super Token, aiming to earn liquidation rewards.

behaviors.name: Super App Reacts to Stream Creation
behaviors.description: The Superfluid Host contract invokes a callback (e.g., afterAgreementCreated) on a registered Super App when a stream is created to/from it.

behaviors.name: User Grants Flow Permissions
behaviors.description: A user grants permissions to a Flow Operator to manage their streams for a specific Super Token.

behaviors.name: Flow Operator Manages Stream
behaviors.description: A Flow Operator, using granted permissions, creates, updates, or deletes a stream on behalf of the original sender.

behaviors.name: User Queries Superfluid Subgraph
behaviors.description: A user or dApp sends a GraphQL query to the Superfluid Subgraph to retrieve indexed data about protocol activity.

behaviors.name: Auto-Wrap Service Tops Up Super Token Balance
behaviors.description: The Auto-Wrap service detects a low Super Token balance for a user and automatically wraps more underlying ERC20 tokens.

behaviors.name: User Executes Macro via MacroForwarder
behaviors.description: User calls `runMacro` on the MacroForwarder, providing parameters for a specific User-Defined Macro contract to execute a batch of Superfluid operations.
